title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption:
Global citizenship and sustainable development education

An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.

The chart displays index scores for four countries—Colombia, India, Malawi, and Bulgaria—across four education strands in 2020. Colombia and India both show maximum scores of 1 for national education policies and student assessment. Colombia’s education curricula and teacher education score 0.88 and 0.85 respectively, while India’s corresponding values are 0.92 for education curricula and 0.95 for teacher education. Malawi also records a score of 1 for national education policies and student assessment, with education curricula at 0.91 and teacher education at 0.9. Bulgaria’s scores are lower across all categories: teacher education is 0.73, student assessment is 0.71, education curricula is 0.65, and national education policies is 0.56. All bars are rendered in uniform blue, with numerical labels at their ends indicating precise index values. The note clarifies that these indicators are based on self-reporting by countries, though countries are asked to provide supporting evidence to back up their responses. No country shows a score below 0.56, and three of the four countries achieve the maximum score of 1 in at least two categories. The ordering of categories within each country’s panel varies, with no consistent sequence applied across panels.
